mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding month signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.63% provides room for easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.3% is near historical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth reflects consumer confidence and discretionary spending strength. Rising RSAFS signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: expansion phase with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following RSAFS growth > 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth indicates manufacturing strength and capacity utilization expansion. Rising INDPRO signals robust supply-side productivity that supports profit margins and equity valuations over the next 5 trading days in high-frequency trading regimes.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no correlation with INDPRO momentum (Rank IC < 0.02), or directional accuracy falls below 52% over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer wealth effects and construction-driven economic activity. When both series show concurrent strength, aggregate demand expansion produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures non-linear amplification of demand signals.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1400 units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1465.0 thousand units, RSAFS at 757085.0 million
regime_hint: expansion phase with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals wage-price spiral dynamics. When both accelerate simultaneously, central bank tightening expectations increase, raising equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.2% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.4%
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159001 thousand, CPIAUCSL at 333.979 index
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in < 58% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, the real interest rate becomes restrictive. This regime produces negative equity returns as the cost of capital rises and growth expectations compress. The mechanism is regime-conditional: it activates only in high-rate, high-inflation environments.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.63%, near restrictive threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where trigger condition is met, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in high-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of aggregate demand. Accelerating quarterly GDP growth signals expanding economic capacity and rising corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This is an always-on trend signal with long horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 24152.656 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with GDPC1 growth rates (Rank IC < 0.04), or directional accuracy falls below 53% over any 3-year rolling window.
